title: Implement backend subscription tier management APIs (list_tiers/get_tier + admin CRUD)

requirements.text: In backend/main.mo, add a new public type `SubscriptionTier` with fields: `id: Text`, `name: Text`, `maxBots: ?Nat` (null means unlimited), `maxApiCalls: ?Nat` (null means unlimited), `priceInCents: Nat`, `features: [Text]`, `active: Bool`.

quotes: I need: 1. A SubscriptionTier type with fields: id (Text), name (Text), maxBots (Nat), maxApiCalls (Nat), priceInCents (Nat), features ([Text]), active (Bool).

acceptanceCriteria: The canister compiles and exposes `public type SubscriptionTier = { ... }` exactly with the requested fields.
`maxBots` and `maxApiCalls` are optional types (`?Nat`) where `null` represents unlimited.

requirements.text: Persist subscription tiers in stable storage keyed by `id`, and maintain an in-memory lookup map keyed by `id` for efficient reads/writes. Do not remove or change any legacy public types/methods listed in the file header compatibility inventory.

quotes: 2. A stable map of tiers keyed by id.
Preserve ALL legacy public methods listed in the file header comment

acceptanceCriteria: Tier data survives canister upgrade (stable storage is used).
Tier lookup is keyed by `id` and supports create, update, toggle active, get, and list operations.
No legacy public method signatures/types are removed or renamed.

requirements.text: Seed default tiers during initialization if the stable tier store is empty: `free` (name "Paper Hand", maxBots= ?1, maxApiCalls=?1000, priceInCents=0, active=true), `pro` (name "Diamond Hand", maxBots=?5, maxApiCalls=?50000, priceInCents=5000, active=true), `whale` (name "Whale", maxBots=null, maxApiCalls=null, priceInCents=50000, active=true).

quotes: 3. Default tiers seeded on init: "free" (Paper Hand, 1 bot, 1000 calls, 0 cents), "pro" (Diamond Hand, 5 bots, 50000 calls, 5000 cents), "whale" (Whale, unlimited, unlimited, 50000 cents).

acceptanceCriteria: On first deploy with no existing tier state, calling `list_tiers()` returns at least the three default tiers with the specified IDs and values.
On upgrades where tiers already exist, defaults are not duplicated or overwritten.

requirements.text: Implement public query methods expected by the frontend: `list_tiers()` returning `Result<[SubscriptionTier]>` and `get_tier(id : Text)` returning `Result<SubscriptionTier>`. These methods must be publicly accessible (not admin-only) and must return variants matching the existing `Result` type pattern (`#ok` / `#err`) so the frontend can rely on `__kind__ === 'ok'` / `__kind__ === 'err'` decoding.

quotes:
The frontend already calls these methods that don't exist yet:
  actor.list_tiers()
  actor.get_tier(id)

5. list_tiers returns Result<[SubscriptionTier]> (the frontend expects __kind__ === 'ok' pattern).
6. get_tier returns Result<SubscriptionTier>.
2. list_tiers / get_tier = public query; create/update/delete = admin-only

acceptanceCriteria: `list_tiers` is a `public query` method and returns `#ok([SubscriptionTier])` on success and `#err(Error)` on failure.
`get_tier` is a `public query` method and returns `#ok(SubscriptionTier)` when the tier exists and `#err(Error)` when it does not.
Returned values are encoded using the existing `Result<T>` variant style used elsewhere in main.mo (`#ok` / `#err`).

requirements.text: Add admin-only tier management update calls using AccessControl.isAdmin for authorization checks: `create_tier(tier : SubscriptionTier)`, `update_tier(id : Text, patch : { name : ?Text; maxBots : ??Nat; maxApiCalls : ??Nat; priceInCents : ?Nat; features : ?[Text]; active : ?Bool })` (partial update semantics), and `toggle_tier_active(id : Text, active : Bool)`. All mutations must be `shared` update calls (not query). `create_tier` must reject duplicate `id` with `#err(...)` and must not overwrite existing tiers.

quotes: 4. Admin-only CRUD: create_tier, update_tier, list_tiers, toggle_tier_active, get_tier.
All mutations must be update calls, never query calls
Access control: use AccessControl.hasPermission for user checks, AccessControl.isAdmin for admin checks

3. update_tier = partial update (optional fields, null = keep existing)
4. create_tier = reject duplicates (#err), use update_tier for changes

acceptanceCriteria: Non-admin callers receive an authorization failure (`#err(#Unauthorized)` or an equivalent `#err(Error)` outcome consistent with the project’s Result/Error patterns) for create/update/toggle operations.
`create_tier` returns `#err(...)` if a tier with the same `id` already exists; no overwrite occurs.
`update_tier` performs partial updates: omitted/`null` patch fields do not change existing values; the method does not require sending a full tier record.
`toggle_tier_active` updates only the `active` field for the specified tier ID.
All tier mutations are implemented as update calls (`public shared ({ caller }) func ...`).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo (single-canister architecture).
Preserve ALL legacy public methods and public types listed in backend/main.mo header compatibility inventory; do not rename or remove them.
All mutations must be update calls (never query calls).
Use AccessControl.isAdmin for admin checks (and AccessControl.hasPermission for user checks if needed).
Do not modify frontend immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui/*.
Keep files under 1000 lines; if needed, split new tier logic into new Motoko modules and import them from backend/main.mo (without altering legacy API).
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Implementing Stripe payment flows or subscription checkout UI.
Adding third-party authentication (anything other than Internet Identity).
Adding a delete_tier method unless explicitly requested (current scope includes create/update/toggle/get/list only).
Changing existing license, trade, bot, or audit-log behaviors beyond what is necessary to add tier storage and APIs.
